title: A friendly invite to anyone helping teams work better in monday.com
excerpt:
If you’ve ever set up a monday.com account for yourself or someone else—and wondered if you missed a step—this session is for you. 
Whether you’re building workflows for clients, managing a team, or just getting started,…

posts.author: Sammy (Samantha)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’ve ever set up a monday.com account for yourself or someone else—and wondered if you missed a step—this session is for you.

Whether you’re building workflows for clients, managing a team, or just getting started, we’ll walk you through the key building blocks we use to help real teams go from scattered spreadsheets to a setup that actually works (and keeps working).

This is part of our Tuesdays with monday series—short, practical sessions packed with real examples and simple strategies.

Here’s what we’ll cover:

Why discovery calls are worth it (and who to invite)
How mondayDocs and WorkCanvas help you align before you build
Turning real-life workflows into useful board structures
Adoption tips to keep things running smoothly after go-live

We’ll also show live demos and leave plenty of time for Q&A at the end.

Who should join?

  • Consultants who help clients build smarter
  • Internal champions trying to make monday work across teams
  • New users who want to start strong
  • Anyone who’s ever inherited a messy monday account and said, “There’s got to be a better way…”

Have a question already? Email us at sam@purpleplumtech.com
Can’t make it live? Register anyway and we’ll send the recording

Register here:

We’d love to see you there. Let’s build something better—together.

posts.author: mosinjack (mosin jack)
posts.cookedHtml: This sounds like the kind of session my messy monday boards have been crying out for Love that you’re covering everything from discovery calls to adoption tips—those little things make a big difference. Can’t wait to soak up some workflow wisdom and finally tame the chaos!

posts.author: Sammy (Samantha)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @mosinjack! Hope to see you at the webinar tomorrow!
